response:
Task  
Sarah owns a small retail shop that supports local factory workers by selling their handmade products. She decides to run a promotional week where she offers a 15% discount on all items produced by the factory workers. If a customer purchases a handcrafted table originally priced at $200 and a set of factory-made chairs originally priced at $150, how much does the customer pay for these items during the promotional week? Remember to apply the discount to each item separately.  

***  
Task  
The customer will pay the discounted price for each item individually. The handcrafted table, originally priced at $200, will be reduced by 15%, resulting in a discounted price of $170. The factory-made chairs, originally priced at $150, will also be reduced by 15%, resulting in a discounted price of $127.50.  

***  
Task  
Reach the final amount by calculating the discounted prices for both items and adding them together. The total amount the customer will pay during the promotional week is $170 plus $127.50, which equals $297.50.  

***  
Task  
Milk is a common ingredient in many of the products sold in the shop, and it is also used in the production of the factory-made chairs. Yard is a unit of measurement that is often used to describe the size of the items sold in the shop. The customer pays $297.50 for the items during the promotional week.
